headline: 57% Of Investors Expect Elon Musk's $56B Pay Package To Be Approved As Tesla Inches Closer To Vote: Poll
summary: Survey finds investors expect Tesla CEO Elon Musk's 2018 pay package to be reinstated by shareholder vote.

headline: Nvidia's Meteoric Rise Brings It Within Striking Distance Of Apple's Market Value: Will Jensen Huang-Led Chip Giant Become World's Second Most Valuable Company?
summary: Nvidia's shares have more than doubled in the first five months of 2024, while Apple's stock has seen little movement.

headline: Netflix Co-Founder Says You Don't Need MBA Or Investors To Start A Business: 'Search For Pain… And Fall In Love With Trying To Solve Problems'
summary: Netflix co-founder Marc Randolph emphasized that business success relies on a tolerance for risk, passion, and a good idea rather than an MBA, Silicon Valley, or investors.

headline: Elon Musk Reveals Tesla's FSD Will Have Three Modes For Drivers To Choose From: 'Chill, Standard And Hurry'
summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk on Friday said that its full self-driving (FSD) driver assistance software will have three modes in the future, namely 'chill,' 'standard,' and 'hurry.'

headline: Tesla Fires Back At Hostile Proxy Advisory Firms, Rivian's 'Messy' Q2 Warning, Faraday Future Stock Deflates And More: Biggest EV Stories Of The Week
summary: Tesla's next catalyst is likely to be its annual shareholder meeting scheduled for June 13, while Rivian warned of near-term softness.

headline: Nio Leads Strong May Deliveries By Chinese EV Makers — Do These Numbers Have Implications for Tesla?
summary: China has become a highly competitive market for EVs, and companies are fighting tooth and nail to thrive.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Nvidia, Tesla, Alphabet And Shiba Inu investor Turns $2,625 Into $1.1M
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors’ favorite stocks over the last week — here’s a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Elon Musk Faces $7.5B Insider Trading Allegation From Tesla Shareholder
summary: Tesla Inc. shareholder Michael Perry on Thursday filed a lawsuit against Elon Musk alleging insider trading when the CEO sold shares worth over $7.5 billion in the last two months of 2022.

headline: Tesla's Humanoid Bot Optimus Guides Shareholders On Voting Process Ahead Of Key June Meeting
